description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline using the ruby:${RUBY_VERSION} Docker image (default RUBY_VERSION 3.3) with a vendor cache keyed by ruby-${RUBY_VERSION} and the gitlab-org runner tag. Define stages test, dast, and deploy; in the test stage add an rspec job that runs the shared before_script (git config, bundle config, conditional BUNDLER_VERSION export, bundle install) then executes `bundle exec rspec spec -f d -c` across a parallel matrix of RUBY_VERSION values 3.0-3.4; also add an rspec_integration job with the same before_script and matrix, runs `bundle exec rspec spec -t integration -f d -c`, and starts redis:${REDIS_VERSION} as redis-master and a bitnamilegacy/redis-sentinel:${REDIS_SENTINEL_VERSION} sentinel service configured via an inline shell command, exposing REDIS_SENTINELS, REDIS_SENTINEL_PASSWORD, REDIS_SENTINEL_URL, REDIS_SENTINEL_USERNAME, REDIS_URL, and REDIS_VERSION variables; add a rubocop job that runs `bundle exec rubocop` after the shared before_script. Include security jobs gemnasium-dependency_scanning and secret_detection that trigger only on merge requests, the default branch, or tags (using the same rule set), and pull in the container-scanning, sast, secret-detection, DAST and Dependency-Scanning templates as well as the gem-release component for deployment. The workflow follows the same rules, ensuring the pipeline runs only for MRs, default-branch commits, or tags, and the gem-release component will publish the gem to RubyGems in the deploy stage.
